target.title: fix(cron): preserve provider/model on isolated-run timeout row
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When an isolated cron agent run fails on a timeout/cancel, the persisted `cron_run_logs` row drops the already-resolved `provider`, `model`, and `session_id`. Operators see an un-attributed `error  ...  error=cron: job execution timed out (last phase: tool-execution-started)` row next to successful `openai gpt-5.4 <session>` rows, which makes a concrete model/tool failure look like a generic pre-session cron timeout and forces transcript/live-log spelunking to recover the model path.

Closes #95873

## Why This Change Was Made

The reporter's exact row text — `cron: job execution timed out (last phase: ...)` — is produced only by `timeoutErrorMessage()` (`src/cron/service/execution-errors.ts:15`), fired by the cron agent watchdog (`src/cron/service/agent-watchdog.ts:85`) and returned by the **timer-built** timeout outcome in `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` (`src/cron/service/timer.ts`). The persisted run-log row is mapped from that returned `coreResult` (`src/cron/service/ops.ts:945-972` → `src/gateway/server-cron.ts:632`).

`executeJobCoreWithTimeout` builds **three** error outcomes that bypass the inner run result (the inner result loses the `Promise.race` and is discarded), and each one is what reaches the row:

1. **Timed wall-clock timeout** (`jobTimeoutMs` set) — the watchdog accumulates the resolved run identity into `activeExecution` via the existing `onExecutionStarted`/`onExecutionPhase` wiring (`run.ts:1407`/`1421`, sourced from `prepared.context.liveSelection`), and the timeout outcome now spreads it in.
2. **Timed operator-cancel** (`jobTimeoutMs` set) — same watchdog snapshot, carried into `createOperatorCancellationOutcome(watchdog.activeExecution())`.
3. **Timeout-disabled operator-cancel** (`timeoutSeconds: 0`, no watchdog) — there is no watchdog to snapshot the identity, and the branch did not even wire the execution callbacks, so the cancel outcome returned blank. This path now wires the same `onExecutionStarted`/`onExecutionPhase` callbacks, accumulates the resolved identity locally, and carries it into the cancel outcome.

Pre-runner setup timeouts and the gateway-restart abort (no resolved model yet) intentionally stay blank.

The first commit's `run.ts` catch change is kept: it covers the sibling path where the inner result *does* reach the row (cron-nested lane backstop / unexpected throw, when the core promise wins the race). Together these surfaces cover timeout, lane-backstop, and both post-runner cancel branches.

## User Impact

Timed-out and post-runner-cancelled isolated cron runs — including `timeoutSeconds: 0` (no-timeout) runs cancelled by an operator — now record their resolved `provider`, `model`, and `session_id` in `cron_run_logs`, so the failure row reads as an attributed model failure instead of a blank cron timeout. Observability-only: no change to run success/failure outcomes, delivery, or session lifecycle. Distinct from the underlying `missing_tool_result` failure that caused the report's timeout.

## Evidence

**Unit (real `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` boundary + negative control, both cancel branches).** Two timer-regression tests drive the real boundary: one fires a watchdog timeout against a runner that reports the resolved identity then hangs; the other fires an operator cancel against a `timeoutSeconds: 0` runner that reports identity then hangs. Both assert the returned outcome carries provider/model/session. Removing each path's spread reproduces the bug exactly:

```text
AssertionError: expected undefined to be 'deepseek'
 src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts  keeps resolved provider/model/session on timeout-disabled cancel rows (#95873)
```

With the fix restored: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` -> `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 66 passed (66)`.

**Live before/after (real gateway + real cron + persisted `cron_run_logs` SQLite row).** Real `openclaw gateway` from a clean `pnpm build`; a stub provider whose model resolves from the static catalog but whose `/chat/completions` hangs, so the run resolves `provider`/`model`, enters the runner, then stalls until the cron watchdog fires — reproducing the reporter's `(last phase: ...)` timeout row. Read straight from `state/openclaw.sqlite` `cron_run_logs`:

```text
BEFORE (fix stashed + rebuilt):
  error       = cron: job execution timed out (last phase: model-call-started)
  provider    = null
  model       = null
  session_id  = null

AFTER (fix):
  error       = cron: job execution timed out (last phase: attempt-dispatch)
  provider    = stub
  model       = good
  session_id  = 7d9f8938-0da7-4324-9f8d-4c72730b626c
```

`stub`/`good` is the deterministic hang-provider used to force a reliable post-runner watchdog timeout; the point is that the attribution fields survive on the persisted timeout row.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` (66 passed; includes the new #95873 timed-timeout and timeout-disabled-cancel cases, each with a negative control)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/ops.regression.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.test.ts src/cron/service/agent-watchdog.test.ts` (30 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.meta-error-status.test.ts` (5 passed)
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` (clean); `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` (clean)
- Live: real gateway + cron run-log SQLite before/after as above

## Risk checklist

Low risk, observability-only. `timer.ts` +15/-2: a pure attribution helper plus spreading already-resolved `provider`/`model`/`session` from watchdog-visible (timed branch) and locally-accumulated (timeout-disabled branch) execution state into the timeout and post-runner cancel outcomes. No change to run status, error text, delivery, or session lifecycle; pre-runner setup timeouts and the gateway-restart abort remain blank by design.

AI-assisted: drafted and verified with AI assistance; all evidence was run locally.
target.author: zengwen-dt
